hadith_thaqalayn.text: انی تارک فیکم الثقلین... کتاب الله و عترتی
hadith_thaqalayn.islamweb: Confirmed in al-Mustadrak and major collections
hadith_thaqalayn.actual_grade: Sahih — in Sahih Muslim (highest canonical status)
hadith_thaqalayn.notes: No dispute on authenticity. The debate is purely on meaning.

hadith_safinah.text: مثل أهل بیتی فیکم مثل سفینة نوح من رکبها نجی ومن تخلف عنها غرق
hadith_safinah.islamweb: Found in al-Mustadrak (al-Hakim: sahih on Muslim's conditions). Chain: Maymun → Ahmad → Yunus → al-Mufaddal → Abu Ishaq → Hanash → Abu Dharr
kingoflinks_sources: al-Fakihi, Akhbar Makkah vol.3 p.112 — chain: Isma'il → al-Mufaddal → Abu Ishaq → Hanash → Abu Dharr
al-Dulabi, al-Kuna wa al-Asma vol.1 p.232 — chain: Rawh → Yahya → Abd al-Karim → Aslam al-Makki → Abu al-Tufayl
al-Bazzar, Musnad vol.9 p.343 (#3900) — chain: Amr → Muslim ibn Ibrahim → al-Hasan ibn Abi Ja'far → Ali ibn Zayd → Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab → Abu Dharr
al-Tabari (Muhibb), Dhakha'ir al-Uqba p.20 — from Ibn Abbas and Ali
al-Mizzi, Tahdhib al-Kamal vol.28 p.411 — via Abu Ya'la → Suwayd → al-Mufaddal → Abu Ishaq → Hanash → Abu Dharr
al-Dhahabi, Mizan al-I'tidal vol.1 p.482 — via al-Hasan ibn Abi Ja'far chain
al-Salihi al-Shami, Subul al-Huda wa al-Rashad vol.10 p.490 — via al-Hakim from Abu Dharr
al-Zarandi al-Hanafi, Nazm Durar al-Simtayn p.235 — via Abu al-Tufayl from Abu Dharr
hadith_safinah.actual_grade: Multiple chains, authenticated by al-Hakim. Some individual chains have weak narrators but collectively the hadith has multiple routes
hadith_safinah.verdict: CONFIRMED in al-Mustadrak (verified via islamweb.net). Mir Hamid Husain in Abaqat Vol 23 cites 40 Sunni scholars with named books and chains. Not in the 6 canonical collections (Sunnah.com scope). Kingoflinks claims 56 Sunni sources — these are leads requiring independent verification from the actual Sunni books.
hadith_safinah.notes: Ibn Taymiyyah in Minhaj al-Sunnah denies any isnad exists. Mir Hamid Husain cites 40 Sunni scholars who narrated it. Kingoflinks documents 9 specific sources with full chains.

kingoflinks_detailed_sources.author: al-Fakihi
kingoflinks_detailed_sources.chain: Isma'il → al-Mufaddal → Abu Ishaq → Hanash → Abu Dharr

kingoflinks_detailed_sources.author: al-Dulabi
kingoflinks_detailed_sources.chain: Rawh → Yahya → Abd al-Karim → Aslam al-Makki → Abu al-Tufayl

kingoflinks_detailed_sources.author: al-Bazzar
kingoflinks_detailed_sources.chain: Amr → Muslim ibn Ibrahim → al-Hasan ibn Abi Ja'far → Ali ibn Zayd → Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab → Abu Dharr

kingoflinks_detailed_sources.author: al-Mizzi
kingoflinks_detailed_sources.chain: Abu Ya'la → Suwayd → al-Mufaddal → Abu Ishaq → Hanash → Abu Dharr

kingoflinks_detailed_sources.author: al-Zarandi al-Hanafi
kingoflinks_detailed_sources.chain: via Abu al-Tufayl from Abu Dharr

al_mustadrak.author: al-Hakim al-Naysaburi
al_mustadrak.chain: Maymun → Ahmad → Yunus → al-Mufaddal → Abu Ishaq → Hanash → Abu Dharr

unverified_leads.source: kingoflinks.net (Shia polemical website — NOT authoritative for Sunni hadith)
unverified_leads.note: Book names listed are real Sunni books, but specific references need independent verification from the actual books or from authoritative Sunni databases like islamweb.net

hadith_iqtidau.text: اقتدوا بالذین من بعدی ابی بکر و عمر
hadith_iqtidau.islamweb: Tirmidhi: 'هذا حديث حسن'. Commentary notes tadlis by Sufyan ibn Uyayna in the chain. NO tawatur claimed by the commentators.
hadith_iqtidau.dehlavi_grade: Claims 'بدرجه شهرت و تواتر معنوی رسیده' (reached the level of fame and tawatur in meaning)
hadith_iqtidau.actual_grade: Hasan only (Tirmidhi). NOT in Sahihayn. Chain has known issues (tadlis). No hadith scholar classifies it as mutawatir.
hadith_iqtidau.verdict: OVERSTATED — exists but Dehlavi inflates Hasan to tawatur without evidence
hadith_iqtidau.notes: This is Dehlavi's most overstated claim. He says it reached tawatur ma'nawi but no muhaddith supports this. Al-Tirmidhi only says 'hasan' and notes chain issues.

hadith_humayra.text: خذوا شطر دینکم عن هذه الحمیراء
hadith_humayra.islamweb: Islamweb fatwa #12234: FABRICATED (موضوع). Ibn Hajar: 'لا أعرف له إسناداً ولا رأیته في شیء من کتب الحدیث' (I know no chain for it and have not seen it in any hadith book). Al-Mizzi and al-Dhahabi: 'did not recognize it'. Al-Shawkani lists it in al-Fawa'id al-Majmu'ah fi al-Ahadith al-Mawdu'ah.
hadith_humayra.actual_grade: FABRICATED (mawdu') — declared so by Ibn Hajar, al-Dhahabi, al-Mizzi, al-Sakhawi, al-Shawkani
hadith_humayra.verdict: FABRICATED — Dehlavi calls sahih what the greatest Sunni hadith scholars call fabricated
hadith_humayra.notes: This is Dehlavi's most serious evidentiary failure. He presents a hadith declared fabricated by his own tradition's greatest scholars as 'sahih' without any source reference.

hadith_sunnah_khulafa.text: علیکم بسنتی و سنة الخلفاء الراشدین المهدیین من بعدی
hadith_sunnah_khulafa.sunnah_com: Abu Dawud 4607 (Sahih — al-Albani), Tirmidhi 2676 (Sahih), Ibn Majah 42-43 (Hasan)
hadith_sunnah_khulafa.notes: Legitimate citation but Dehlavi names no source book.

hadith_shura.sunnah_com: Not found as a Prophetic hadith
hadith_shura.dehlavi_grade: Claims 'حدیث صحیح مروی از حضرت امیر که نزد شیعه متواتر است' (sahih narrated from Ali, mutawatir among Shia)
hadith_shura.actual_grade: This is from Ali's letter to Mu'awiya (Nahj al-Balagha), NOT a Prophetic hadith. Dehlavi himself attributes it to Ali, not the Prophet.
hadith_shura.verdict: MISCHARACTERIZED — not a Prophetic hadith; it's Ali's own statement
hadith_shura.notes: Dehlavi uses this cleverly — citing Ali's own words against the Shia position. But calling it 'hadith sahih' is misleading since it's not from the Prophet.

hadith_muadh.text: اعلمکم بالحلال و الحرام معاذ بن جبل
hadith_muadh.notes: Part of a longer hadith listing companions' specialties. Legitimate citation.

hadith_ammar.actual_grade: Hasan — part of the same hadith as the Abu Bakr/Umar one

cross_reference_summary.ibn_taymiyyah_claim: This hadith has no isnad at all, neither sahih nor da'if (Minhaj al-Sunnah)
cross_reference_summary.dehlavi_position: Accepts the hadith but minimizes it to general love/following
cross_reference_summary.mir_hamid_husain_response: Vol 23 cites 40 named Sunni scholars with specific books and chains
cross_reference_summary.kingoflinks_documentation: Claims 56 Sunni sources — Shia polemical website, used for enrichment/leads only. Individual references need independent verification from Sunni authoritative sources.
cross_reference_summary.islamweb_confirmation: Found in al-Mustadrak — al-Hakim grades it sahih on Muslim's conditions
cross_reference_summary.sunnah_com_status: Not in the 18 indexed collections (limited scope — misses al-Mustadrak, al-Tabarani, etc.)
cross_reference_summary.conclusion: Ibn Taymiyyah's claim of 'no isnad' is objectively false. The hadith exists in dozens of Sunni sources with multiple independent chains through companions Abu Dharr, Ibn Abbas, Ali, and Abu Sa'id al-Khudri.

methodology_note: VERIFICATION HIERARCHY: (1) Sunnah.com — Sunni canonical, 18 indexed collections (authoritative). (2) Islamweb.net — Sunni scholarly with fatwas from muhaddithin (authoritative). (3) Kingoflinks.net — Shia polemical website (enrichment/leads ONLY, not authoritative for Sunni hadith grading). Claims from kingoflinks require independent verification from sources (1) or (2) or the actual Sunni books cited.
